core_model.structure_summary: An exact identity stating that the ensemble average of an exponentially weighted functional over a probability measure on paths equals the ratio of two normalizing constants of exponential-family (Gibbs-type) distributions.
core_model.canonical_form: \left\langle e^{-\beta W[\gamma]}\right\rangle = \frac{Z_B}{Z_A} = e^{-\beta\,\Delta F},\qquad P(x)=\frac{e^{-\beta H(x)}}{Z},\quad Z=\int e^{-\beta H(x)}\,dx,\qquad \Delta F \le \langle W\rangle

variables.meaning: path/trajectory in configuration space and its endpoints
scalar functional of a path being exponentially averaged
inverse-scale multiplier in the exponential weighting
probability measure over the path ensemble

deterministic_skeleton.summary: Constrained maximization of an entropy functional over a probability measure, whose stationarity condition yields an exponential-family distribution normalized by a partition function.

notes: Central object is the Jarzynski exponential-average identity derived from a maximum-path-entropy (MaxCaliber) inference principle. The bound Delta F <= <W> follows from convexity (Jensen), motivating convexity_central and variational_bound. The deterministic skeleton is the maximum-entropy variational/stationarity problem producing the Gibbs distribution; could alternatively be tagged 'variational'.
